title: dianthus subacaulis subsp. nivalis
intro:
Dianthus subacaulis subsp. nivalis, also known as the Snow Pink or Alpine Pink, is a rare and delicate species of flowering plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its pink to white, fragrant flowers and slender stems.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Dianthus subacaulis subsp. nivalis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
